LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RON JOHNSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW HE DID NOT CONTROL THE PROPERTY. DEFENDANT DOES NOT AFFIRMATIVELY NEGATE AN ELEMENT OF PREMISES LIABILITY OR NEGLIGENCE AND FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE A NECESSARY ELEMENT OF HIS CLAIMS. TO THE CONTRARY, DEFENDANT HAD EXTENSIVE CONTROL OVER THE BATHROOM, WAS GIVEN A HOME DEPOT CARD FOR REPAIR MATERIALS, WAS ON-CALL TO MAKE REPAIRS WHEN TENANTS HAD ISSUES, AND HAD PREVIOUSLY FIXED A BROKEN URINAL. (DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION.) THE LEAKING URINAL WAS A CHRONIC PROBLEM. (PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION 42:2-11.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery And Related Req. For Sanctions Against The Ccsf
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, DEFENDANT RON JOHNSON's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And request For Sanctions, is taken off calendar, at the request of the moving party. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RON JOHNSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. Defendant fails to show he did not control the property. Defendant does not affirmatively negate an element of premises liability or negligence and fails to establish that plaintiff cannot prove a necessary element of his claims. To the contrary, defendant had extensive control over the bathroom, was given a Home Depot card for repair materials, was on-call to make repairs when tenants had issues, and had previously fixed a broken urinal. (Defendant's deposition.) The leaking urinal was a chronic problem. (Plaintiff's deposition 42:2-11.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
